title: peucedanum aurantiaca
intro: Peucedanum aurantiaca, also known as the orange hog's fennel, is a plant species in the carrot family native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Peucedanum aurantiaca include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Peucedanum aurantiaca can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking, particularly in the form of a bitter-tasting vegetable.
